title: Cleisostoma filiforme
what_description: Cleisostoma filiforme is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid characterized by its slender stems, which are typically 10-20 cm long, and linear, dark green leaves that are 5-15 cm long and 0.5-1.5 cm wide. The plant produces one or two, short-lived, white to yellowish flowers, which are 2-3 cm long and 1.5-2.5 cm wide, with a lip that is 0.5-1 cm long and 1-1.5 cm wide. The flowers are fragrant, with a sweet, honey-like scent, and are pollinated by small bees and wasps. Cleisostoma filiforme is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries such as India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia.

The plant's antioxidant properties are also prominent, as it exhibits scavenging activity, neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress. Additionally, Cleisostoma filiforme exhibits anti-inflammatory activity, which helps in reducing inflammation and alleviating pain. constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Cleisostoma filiforme are flavonoids which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, glycosides which have demonstrated antimicrobial activity, saponins which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and hemolytic activities, phenolic acids which have exhibited antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and alkaloids which have been reported to have antimicrobial and antitumor activities, indicating the plant's potential as a therapeutic agent in treating various health conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Cleisostoma filiforme are infusion, decoction, tincture, tea, and salve. Infusion is a preparation that involves steeping the plant's flowers or leaves in hot water to release their bioactive compounds, which are said to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Decoction is a preparation that involves boiling the plant's roots or rhizomes in water to release their bioactive compounds, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. Tincture is a concentrated liquid preparation made by soaking the plant's flowers or leaves in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, which is used to treat various health issues, including anxiety and insomnia. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the plant's flowers or leaves in a carrier oil, which is used to treat various skin issues, including wounds and eczema.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Cleisostoma filiforme improperly include diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, and stomach discomfort, due to the high levels of bioactive compounds such as iridoids and alkaloids, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and alter normal gut function, leading to these symptoms. The improper use of Cleisostoma filiforme can also cause excessive stimulation of the central nervous system, resulting in dizziness and nausea. Furthermore, the high levels of alkaloids can induce vomiting, as the body attempts to expel the toxins. Ingestion of large amounts of Cleisostoma filiforme can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes and fluids, causing diarrhea and dehydration. It is essential to use this plant in moderation and under the guidance of a medical professional to avoid these side effects.
